primary_policy_tool: Unilateral signaling instead of a standing tariff: an approved-then-suspended 15% duty, plus import license halts
outcome_summary: Dangote Refinery ramp-up (85%+ utilization by mid-2025) is displacing imports faster than any tariff action. PMS import bill fell 42% in 2025.

primary_policy_tool: Sustained tariff differential (0-10% for CKD/SKD kits vs 35-40% fully-built)
outcome_summary: Tariff wall has produced many licensed assemblers, but only ~29% aggregate capacity utilization. That's protection without competitiveness.

primary_policy_tool: Sustained high tariff plus intermittent import bans and waivers (2019 border closure, 2024 duty-free window)
outcome_summary: ~70-76% self-sufficiency achieved, but persistent large-scale smuggling shows the tariff wall is porous.
